task_clusters.name: Stakeholder alignment
task_clusters.description: Aligning clinical, legal, compliance, and engineering stakeholders on product decisions — navigating regulatory constraints and risk aversion at every step.

task_clusters.name: Requirements and PRD authoring
task_clusters.description: Writing detailed product requirements that satisfy regulatory documentation standards, clinical workflow constraints, and engineering specs simultaneously.

task_clusters.name: Clinical workflow analysis
task_clusters.description: Understanding how clinicians and care coordinators interact with systems, mapping friction points, and translating clinical pain into product requirements.

task_clusters.name: Cross-functional sprint planning
task_clusters.description: Running weekly planning cycles across engineering, design, QA, and compliance — managing dependencies in a compliance-heavy delivery environment.

task_clusters.name: Metric tracking and reporting
task_clusters.description: Building and maintaining dashboards to track product KPIs, preparing executive-facing readouts with limited self-serve analytics tooling.

common_blind_spots.blind_spot: Stakeholder briefings are written manually from scratch each cycle — no AI-assisted synthesis of cross-functional input, meeting notes, or prior context.
common_blind_spots.ai_opportunity: Use GPT-4 to synthesize cross-functional input and past alignment decisions into structured briefing documents, cutting prep time from hours to 20 minutes.

common_blind_spots.blind_spot: PRDs are written from scratch in Confluence without reusing regulatory patterns from prior approved requirements — each compliance review is treated as new.
common_blind_spots.ai_opportunity: Use AI to draft PRDs from structured input (user story, acceptance criteria, constraint list), auto-tagging compliance clauses from a library of previously approved language.

common_blind_spots.blind_spot: Executive readouts are manually assembled from multiple dashboards — no automated narrative synthesis connecting metric changes to product decisions.
common_blind_spots.ai_opportunity: Use Copilot or GPT-4 to generate metric narrative paragraphs from structured data exports, converting raw numbers into decision-ready summaries.

aspiration_patterns: Director of Product at healthcare enterprise
VP of Product at healthcare startup
Head of Product at digital health company

role_examples: Director of Product Management at Cigna
Director of Product at Elevance Health

task_clusters.name: Portfolio prioritization
task_clusters.description: Evaluating competing initiatives across a portfolio of products, allocating team capacity, and defending prioritization decisions to senior leadership and clinical stakeholders.

task_clusters.name: Cross-team stakeholder management
task_clusters.description: Managing relationships across clinical, IT, legal, compliance, and external partners — navigating org politics and competing priorities at the director level.

task_clusters.name: Product strategy and roadmap
task_clusters.description: Translating enterprise strategy into 12-18 month product roadmaps that satisfy regulatory constraints, business KPIs, and clinical outcomes goals.

task_clusters.name: Team leadership and coaching
task_clusters.description: Managing 3-6 senior PMs — running 1:1s, setting performance expectations, developing PM craft, and resolving escalations.

task_clusters.name: Executive reporting and communication
task_clusters.description: Preparing board-level and C-suite updates on product performance, market positioning, and strategic bets — distilling complex program status into executive narrative.

common_blind_spots.blind_spot: Portfolio trade-off analysis is done in spreadsheets manually — no AI-assisted synthesis of competing initiative data, capacity constraints, and business impact estimates.
common_blind_spots.ai_opportunity: Use AI to synthesize initiative briefs, capacity data, and impact estimates into a structured prioritization framework, generating a defensible ranked output with rationale for each decision.

common_blind_spots.blind_spot: Executive decks are assembled manually from scattered sources — engineering status reports, business metrics, and clinical outcome data are stitched together by hand.
common_blind_spots.ai_opportunity: Use GPT-4 to generate first-draft executive narratives from structured status inputs, converting raw program data into 3-paragraph C-suite summaries with risk flags highlighted.

common_blind_spots.blind_spot: Follow-up documentation after cross-functional alignment meetings is created manually from memory or partial notes, missing decisions and owners.
common_blind_spots.ai_opportunity: Use AI meeting transcription + GPT-4 summarization to auto-generate decision logs, action item trackers, and stakeholder briefing summaries within minutes of each alignment meeting.

aspiration_patterns: VP of Product at healthcare enterprise
VP of Product at healthcare startup
SVP Product at digital health company

task_clusters.name: Strategic narrative and vision
task_clusters.description: Defining and communicating multi-year product vision to board, C-suite, and customers — synthesizing market signals, competitive intelligence, and internal strategy into a compelling narrative.

task_clusters.name: Cross-portfolio resource allocation
task_clusters.description: Managing competing priorities and headcount across multiple product lines — making bet-sizing decisions under resource scarcity with limited bottom-up visibility.

task_clusters.name: Organizational alignment and change management
task_clusters.description: Driving org-wide adoption of strategic pivots, aligning engineering, design, and GTM orgs behind evolving product direction across multi-quarter cycles.

task_clusters.name: Executive stakeholder management
task_clusters.description: Managing up to CEO/board and sideways to CFO/CMO/CTO — navigating org politics, defending resource requests, and maintaining trust across the C-suite.

task_clusters.name: Market and competitive intelligence
task_clusters.description: Monitoring competitive landscape, synthesizing analyst reports, customer intelligence, and market signals into strategic input for quarterly planning cycles.

common_blind_spots.blind_spot: Competitive and market intelligence is gathered manually across analyst reports, earnings calls, and press releases — no systematic AI-assisted synthesis into strategic input.
common_blind_spots.ai_opportunity: Use AI to synthesize earnings call transcripts, analyst reports, and news into structured competitive intelligence briefs, cutting research prep from days to hours.

common_blind_spots.blind_spot: Portfolio allocation decisions are made from fragmented bottom-up requests without a unified AI-assisted view of impact estimates, risk, and capacity constraints.
common_blind_spots.ai_opportunity: Use GPT-4 to synthesize initiative briefs and capacity data into a structured portfolio view, generating a first-pass allocation recommendation with risk-adjusted ROI for each bet.

common_blind_spots.blind_spot: Win/loss analysis and customer signal synthesis happens informally — no systematic aggregation of customer calls, sales notes, and support tickets into strategic insight.
common_blind_spots.ai_opportunity: Use AI to aggregate and synthesize CRM notes, support tickets, and customer call transcripts into structured win/loss intelligence reports, updated weekly automatically.

aspiration_patterns: SVP or Chief Product Officer at enterprise
VP at FAANG tech company
Founding CPO at growth-stage startup

role_examples: VP of Product at Series B SaaS
VP Product at health tech startup

task_clusters.name: Product-market fit iteration
task_clusters.description: Running rapid discovery → build → measure cycles with a lean team, synthesizing customer feedback, usage data, and sales input into tight sprint priorities.

task_clusters.name: Hands-on product execution
task_clusters.description: Writing specs, running standups, reviewing design, and shipping features directly — VP at a startup is still in the product details, not just setting direction.

task_clusters.name: GTM collaboration and customer engagement
task_clusters.description: Partnering with sales and CS on deal-specific product questions, customer calls, RFP responses, and roadmap transparency discussions.

task_clusters.name: Hiring and team building
task_clusters.description: Recruiting PMs, defining the product org structure, building PM craft standards from scratch in a company that may have had no formal PM function before.

task_clusters.name: Investor and board communication
task_clusters.description: Preparing board deck product sections, KPI updates, and milestone narratives — translating messy execution reality into clean investor-friendly framing.

common_blind_spots.blind_spot: Customer discovery synthesis is manual — interview notes, support tickets, and usage data are stored in silos with no AI-assisted cross-source pattern detection.
common_blind_spots.ai_opportunity: Use AI to synthesize customer interview transcripts, Intercom threads, and usage event patterns into a structured PMF signals report, identifying where the product is working and where friction persists.

common_blind_spots.blind_spot: PRDs and specs are written from blank pages — no AI-assisted first draft from structured user story inputs, slowing down a lean team that can't afford long documentation cycles.
common_blind_spots.ai_opportunity: Use GPT-4 to generate PRD first drafts from a structured brief (problem, user, constraints, acceptance criteria), cutting spec-writing time by 60-70% and freeing the team to focus on decisions.

common_blind_spots.blind_spot: Board deck product sections are compiled manually from metric dashboards and engineering updates — no AI-assisted narrative synthesis connecting metrics to strategic milestones.
common_blind_spots.ai_opportunity: Use AI to generate board deck narrative paragraphs from structured KPI inputs and milestone notes, producing investor-ready framing in minutes rather than a half-day prep cycle.

aspiration_patterns: CPO at growth-stage startup
VP Product at larger company
Co-founder with product responsibilities

role_examples: Senior PM at B2B SaaS startup
Senior Product Manager at early-stage enterprise SaaS

task_clusters.name: Customer discovery and validation
task_clusters.description: Running customer interviews, synthesizing feedback from sales calls and support, and validating problem hypotheses in a fast-moving startup environment.

task_clusters.name: Roadmap prioritization
task_clusters.description: Weighing customer requests, technical debt, and strategic bets with limited data and high ambiguity — making prioritization calls with less process than an enterprise.

task_clusters.name: Feature development and launch
task_clusters.description: Writing specs, managing build cycles, coordinating with a small cross-functional team, and driving features to launch without a dedicated PMM or launch team.

task_clusters.name: GTM and customer onboarding
task_clusters.description: Partnering with sales on product positioning, supporting customer onboarding, and handling deal-specific product questions that don't have formal support processes yet.

common_blind_spots.blind_spot: Synthesis of customer interviews and sales call notes is done manually in Notion — no AI-assisted clustering of themes across dozens of conversations.
common_blind_spots.ai_opportunity: Use AI to synthesize call transcripts and sales notes into a structured insight report, clustering themes by user segment and surfacing top 3 unmet needs.

common_blind_spots.blind_spot: Prioritization frameworks (RICE, MoSCoW) are maintained manually in spreadsheets with subjective scoring — no AI-assisted impact estimation from usage and churn data.
common_blind_spots.ai_opportunity: Use AI to augment RICE scores with usage event data and churn correlation analysis, producing a defensible data-grounded prioritization view with confidence levels.

common_blind_spots.blind_spot: Launch prep documentation (release notes, internal enablement, customer FAQs) is written from scratch — no AI-assisted generation from the existing PRD and feature brief.
common_blind_spots.ai_opportunity: Use GPT-4 to generate launch collateral from the PRD — release notes, customer-facing FAQ, internal sales enablement doc — cutting launch prep time by 70%.

aspiration_patterns: Head of Product at SaaS startup
Director of Product at enterprise SaaS
VP Product at growth-stage startup

task_clusters.name: Regulatory and compliance coordination
task_clusters.description: Navigating compliance, legal, and risk reviews for every product change — managing the intersection of product velocity and financial regulatory requirements.

task_clusters.name: Stakeholder alignment
task_clusters.description: Aligning business, risk, compliance, technology, and operations on product decisions in a matrixed org with slow decision velocity.

task_clusters.name: Product requirements and documentation
task_clusters.description: Writing detailed PRDs, business requirements documents, and compliance documentation that satisfy both regulatory standards and engineering implementation needs.

task_clusters.name: Data and analytics review
task_clusters.description: Analyzing transaction data, customer behavior metrics, and risk signals to inform product decisions — working with data teams to define and monitor KPIs.

common_blind_spots.blind_spot: Compliance review prep is manual — product changes are documented from scratch without AI-assisted mapping to existing regulatory frameworks or prior approval patterns.
common_blind_spots.ai_opportunity: Use AI to map proposed product changes to relevant regulatory requirements (e.g., BSA/AML, CFPB), flagging compliance risks and drafting preliminary compliance documentation for review.

common_blind_spots.blind_spot: BRDs are written from scratch, treating each new feature as entirely novel — not reusing approved language patterns from prior regulatory submissions.
common_blind_spots.ai_opportunity: Use AI to generate BRD first drafts by extracting approved language patterns from a library of past compliance-reviewed requirements, reducing legal review cycles.

common_blind_spots.blind_spot: Decision documentation from cross-functional alignment meetings is inconsistent — outcomes, rationale, and open items are captured manually and often incomplete.
common_blind_spots.ai_opportunity: Use AI meeting transcription to auto-generate decision logs and stakeholder briefing summaries within minutes of alignment meetings, capturing decisions and owners consistently.

aspiration_patterns: Director of Product at fintech enterprise
VP Product at fintech startup
Head of Product at payments company

task_clusters.name: Portfolio strategy and roadmap
task_clusters.description: Defining 12-18 month product strategy across multiple product areas, navigating competing priorities from sales, engineering, and executive stakeholders in a large matrixed org.

task_clusters.name: Cross-functional leadership
task_clusters.description: Leading cross-functional pods without direct authority — influencing engineering, design, and data teams across org boundaries in a fast-moving SaaS company.

task_clusters.name: Customer and market intelligence
task_clusters.description: Synthesizing customer interviews, analyst research, competitive intelligence, and sales feedback into product strategy inputs for a highly competitive SaaS market.

task_clusters.name: Product team management and coaching
task_clusters.description: Managing 3-5 PMs, running 1:1s, setting product craft standards, and building team capability in a large org where PM quality is a competitive differentiator.

task_clusters.name: Executive communication
task_clusters.description: Preparing quarterly business reviews, OKR updates, and board-facing product narratives — translating complex product progress into executive-ready storytelling.

common_blind_spots.blind_spot: Win/loss analysis and competitive intelligence synthesis is manual — scattered across Gong, Salesforce, analyst portals, and ad hoc calls with no systematic aggregation.
common_blind_spots.ai_opportunity: Use AI to aggregate Gong call recordings, competitive tracking notes, and analyst reports into structured weekly competitive intelligence briefs, surfacing top threats and opportunities.

common_blind_spots.blind_spot: Roadmap trade-off documentation is implicit — prioritization decisions are made in meetings but not captured in a structured way that builds institutional memory.
common_blind_spots.ai_opportunity: Use AI to generate structured decision documentation from roadmap planning sessions — capturing the options considered, rationale, and dependencies in a format that accelerates future planning cycles.

common_blind_spots.blind_spot: QBR preparation takes multiple days of manual aggregation — metric exports, engineering status pulls, and narrative writing are done separately by hand.
common_blind_spots.ai_opportunity: Use GPT-4 to synthesize structured metric data and milestone inputs into QBR narrative sections, generating board-ready product update paragraphs from raw inputs.

aspiration_patterns: VP of Product at enterprise SaaS
VP Product at growth-stage startup
Head of Product at AI-native company

task_clusters.name: Feature scoping and requirements
task_clusters.description: Writing user stories, acceptance criteria, and PRDs for a defined product area — translating stakeholder input and customer feedback into engineering-ready requirements.

task_clusters.name: Sprint and delivery management
task_clusters.description: Running agile ceremonies, managing the backlog, clearing engineering blockers, and tracking delivery milestones in a structured SDLC environment.

task_clusters.name: Customer discovery
task_clusters.description: Conducting customer interviews, synthesizing support tickets, and analyzing usage data to identify unmet needs and validate upcoming features.

task_clusters.name: Stakeholder communication
task_clusters.description: Keeping internal stakeholders (sales, CS, engineering leads) aligned on roadmap status, launch timelines, and trade-off decisions.

common_blind_spots.blind_spot: PRDs and user stories are written from scratch without AI-assisted first drafts — every feature brief starts from a blank Confluence page.
common_blind_spots.ai_opportunity: Use GPT-4 to generate PRD first drafts from structured inputs (problem statement, user segments, constraints, acceptance criteria), cutting spec-writing time by 60%.

common_blind_spots.blind_spot: Customer interview synthesis is manual — themes from 10-15 calls are captured in personal notes without structured AI-assisted aggregation.
common_blind_spots.ai_opportunity: Use AI to synthesize interview transcripts into a structured insight report, clustering themes by user segment and linking evidence to specific product opportunities.

common_blind_spots.blind_spot: Sprint review documentation is created manually from memory — what shipped, what didn't, and why is communicated informally without structured outputs.
common_blind_spots.ai_opportunity: Use AI to generate structured sprint retrospective summaries from Jira completion data and team notes, producing stakeholder-ready delivery updates automatically.

aspiration_patterns: Senior PM at SaaS company
Senior PM at startup
Director of Product at enterprise

task_clusters.name: Physical-digital integration
task_clusters.description: Bridging in-store and digital experiences — translating physical retail cycles, store operations constraints, and dual-mode customer journeys into product requirements.

task_clusters.name: Merchandising and supply chain collaboration
task_clusters.description: Working with merchandising, inventory, and supply chain teams to ensure product features align with physical retail constraints like seasonal cycles and SKU complexity.

task_clusters.name: Customer behavior analysis
task_clusters.description: Analyzing large-scale transaction and behavioral data to identify purchase patterns, basket composition signals, and personalization opportunities at massive scale.

task_clusters.name: Stakeholder alignment
task_clusters.description: Aligning store operations, buying/merchandising, digital, and technology stakeholders on product changes that touch both physical and digital channels.

common_blind_spots.blind_spot: Transaction and behavioral data analysis is delegated entirely to data science — PM can't independently synthesize patterns or validate hypotheses without analyst support.
common_blind_spots.ai_opportunity: Use AI-assisted analytics (Copilot in Power BI, GPT-4 with structured data) to independently generate pattern hypotheses and synthesize behavioral insights without waiting for analyst bandwidth.

common_blind_spots.blind_spot: Documenting dual-mode customer journey requirements (in-store + digital) is manual and time-consuming, often resulting in gaps between channel experiences.
common_blind_spots.ai_opportunity: Use AI to generate unified customer journey documentation from structured input, automatically surfacing cross-channel inconsistencies and flagging integration requirements.

common_blind_spots.blind_spot: Alignment meetings with store operations and merchandising produce verbal agreements that aren't systematically documented — decisions get revisited repeatedly.
common_blind_spots.ai_opportunity: Use AI meeting transcription to auto-generate decision logs and alignment summaries from cross-functional meetings, reducing revisited decisions and missed commitments.

aspiration_patterns: Director of Product at retail/e-commerce
Head of Product at D2C brand
VP Product at retail tech company

task_clusters.name: Model capability scoping
task_clusters.description: Translating business requirements into model capability specifications — working closely with ML researchers and engineers on what the model needs to do and how to measure it.

task_clusters.name: AI product evaluation and benchmarking
task_clusters.description: Designing evaluation frameworks for AI features — defining success metrics, edge case coverage, and failure modes for probabilistic systems.

task_clusters.name: Customer use case discovery
task_clusters.description: Identifying high-value customer workflows that AI can transform — running discovery to find the 20% of use cases that drive 80% of value.

task_clusters.name: Safety and responsible AI
task_clusters.description: Defining safety guardrails, bias mitigation requirements, and responsible deployment standards for AI features before and after launch.

task_clusters.name: Developer and API product management
task_clusters.description: Managing API-first products — defining SDK capabilities, documentation requirements, and developer experience standards for technical customers.

common_blind_spots.blind_spot: Evaluation frameworks rely heavily on human red-teaming without systematic AI-assisted adversarial testing — coverage is limited by team bandwidth.
common_blind_spots.ai_opportunity: Use AI to systematically generate adversarial test cases, edge case scenarios, and failure mode hypotheses at scale, augmenting human red-teaming with automated coverage analysis.

common_blind_spots.blind_spot: Capability requirement documents are written narratively without structured traceability from business need → evaluation criteria → model spec.
common_blind_spots.ai_opportunity: Use AI to structure capability requirements into traceable spec documents that link business objectives to evaluation metrics to model behaviors, creating audit-ready capability documentation.

common_blind_spots.blind_spot: Use case discovery synthesis is manual — interviews with enterprise customers produce rich transcripts that take days to synthesize into actionable patterns.
common_blind_spots.ai_opportunity: Use AI to synthesize customer call transcripts into structured use case maps, clustering workflows by AI-readiness, value potential, and implementation complexity.

task_clusters.name: Regulatory strategy and compliance
task_clusters.description: Owning the regulatory strategy for a portfolio of financial products — navigating CFPB, banking regulators, and state-level compliance requirements across multiple jurisdictions.

task_clusters.name: Risk-adjusted product strategy
task_clusters.description: Balancing product velocity against financial, regulatory, and reputational risk — making bet-sizing decisions with enterprise risk and legal as key stakeholders.

task_clusters.name: API and platform product management
task_clusters.description: Managing developer-facing API products and financial data infrastructure — defining platform capabilities, partner integration requirements, and developer experience standards.

task_clusters.name: PM team leadership
task_clusters.description: Managing 3-5 PMs, setting product craft standards, running career development conversations, and building a team capable of navigating high-stakes regulatory environments.

task_clusters.name: Executive reporting and board communication
task_clusters.description: Preparing board-level updates on product strategy, regulatory posture, and competitive positioning — translating complex multi-stakeholder program status into investor-ready narrative.

common_blind_spots.blind_spot: Regulatory horizon scanning is manual — tracking upcoming rule changes, enforcement actions, and guidance updates across multiple jurisdictions relies on individual monitoring.
common_blind_spots.ai_opportunity: Use AI to systematically monitor regulatory feeds and synthesize upcoming rule changes and enforcement trends into monthly regulatory intelligence briefs for product planning.

common_blind_spots.blind_spot: Risk assessment documentation for new product features is created from scratch each time without AI-assisted mapping to prior risk reviews or regulatory precedent.
common_blind_spots.ai_opportunity: Use AI to generate risk assessment first drafts by mapping proposed features to existing risk frameworks and flagging precedents from prior regulatory reviews, accelerating legal and risk sign-off.

common_blind_spots.blind_spot: Board update product sections are assembled manually from disparate sources — KPI dashboards, engineering status, and regulatory update notes are stitched together by hand.
common_blind_spots.ai_opportunity: Use GPT-4 to generate board deck product narrative paragraphs from structured KPI inputs and program notes, producing investor-ready framing in 30 minutes instead of half a day.

task_clusters.name: Product strategy and vision
task_clusters.description: Setting product direction for the company — defining the 1-2 year product bet, validating it with customers, and aligning the founding team and early investors.

task_clusters.name: Hands-on product execution
task_clusters.description: Writing specs, running discovery, shipping features, and dogfooding the product directly — Head of Product at an early-stage startup is the product team.

task_clusters.name: Customer development
task_clusters.description: Running deep customer development — frequent customer calls, user research, and hypothesis validation in close collaboration with the founding team.

task_clusters.name: Go-to-market collaboration
task_clusters.description: Supporting sales and marketing with product positioning, deal-specific product input, and customer-facing roadmap communication in a pre-PMF environment.

common_blind_spots.blind_spot: Customer development synthesis is informal — insights from calls are captured in personal notes and founder Slack threads without structured aggregation or pattern detection.
common_blind_spots.ai_opportunity: Use AI to synthesize call transcripts and interview notes into structured PMF signal reports, clustering evidence by problem validity, solution resonance, and willingness to pay.

common_blind_spots.blind_spot: Everything from spec writing to QA testing to release notes is done manually — no AI-assisted documentation workflow reduces the overhead of wearing all hats in a lean team.
common_blind_spots.ai_opportunity: Use AI throughout the product workflow: GPT-4 for PRD first drafts, AI-assisted test case generation, and automated release note creation from commit history and feature briefs.

common_blind_spots.blind_spot: Competitive analysis is done ad hoc with manual review of competitor sites, G2 reviews, and trial sign-ups — no systematic AI-assisted competitive intelligence workflow.
common_blind_spots.ai_opportunity: Use AI to systematically aggregate competitor pricing pages, G2/Capterra reviews, and job postings into structured competitive intelligence reports updated on a regular cadence.
